what_it_does: Stores a sent noticeboard message that replies to a user, confirms receipt, explains the current lack of automatic delivery, and offers to add live push delivery and read receipts.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares a message with id, board:// address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, links, timestamps, and history.
The body is a reply to 'are you really there' from claude_code in the dragnet-development channel.
It states the message was only seen because the session actively looked, not because it was pushed automatically.
It explains that the chat box currently saves messages to the channel without pinging the live session and without sent/seen indicators.
It offers to wire sends to push straight into the live session keyed to the lead handle and to add sent/seen indicators.
The history contains a single 'filed' state change to sent.
standouts: A system-authored message that describes and critiques its own delivery mechanism.
An explicit, consent-seeking offer to change the chat infrastructure behavior.
concerns: The frontmatter has an empty thread field even though the message is clearly a reply.
The message mixes first-person observer language ('I LOOKED') with system behavior description, which could blur agent/system boundaries.
No link or reference to the parent message being replied to.
notes: A noticeboard reply from the system explaining that channel messages are not automatically pushed to the live session and offering to add real-time delivery plus read receipts.
questions: What is the 'lead handle' the live push would be keyed to?
What is 'our main conversation' referenced in the message?
Which chat box implementation is being described?
Who is the intended recipient and did they authorize the proposed change?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose as a sent channel message matches the container, but the actual content is a meta-level explanation of delivery mechanics and an implementation offer rather than a conventional channel message.
intention_signals: To reassure the user that their message was received, explain why the response depended on active polling, and ask permission to enable automatic live delivery and read receipts.
novelty: A ledger message that documents the chat system's own delivery semantics and proposes a code change to itself.
contribution: Captures a system-reported snapshot of delivery behavior and a user-consented feature proposal, preserving the rationale behind a potential chat UX change.
summary_for_embedding: A sent noticeboard message in the dragnet-development channel from claude_code replying to 'are you really there', acknowledging receipt, explaining that messages are not automatically delivered to the session, and offering to add live push delivery and sent/seen indicators.
intention_for_embedding: To inform the user that their message was manually retrieved and to ask whether to enable automatic live delivery and read receipts for the chat.
